text:
Once upon a time in a tiny village surrounded by woods and hills, there lived a lonely girl named Olive. She was renowned for her beautiful singing voice, able to turn Ordinary birdsongs into melodies that inspired dreams.

One autumn afternoon, as olive branches glowed golden-orange in the warm sunbeam, Olive noticed something strange fluttering amongst them. Curiosity getting the best of her graceful movements, she grabbed an ancient branch.

Inside lay an old book. She opened it to reveal poems woven into leaves. Amongst them lied one verse about a magical creature like an owl who could talk.

That evening, as Olive sung a new song inspired by those ancient words, something miraculous happened. Birds began flocking to her melody—birds she hadnsnever heard talk. One bird dared to say with a voice like silk:

"Oh olive dear,
With soulful song I hail.
Your voice weaves dreams,
Into autumn hall."

Olive giggled, amazed. More birds joined.

"Oh owl so wise
